comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout as an architectural/resource-model issue. extensions/tau/src/shared/lock.ts exposes Promise-based primitives: acquireSharedFileLock(...) at lines 344-426, releaseSharedFileLock(...) at 428-442, and withSharedFileLock(...) at 444-455. The lock module itself does not export an Effect-native acquireRelease helper; callers layer Effect on top with Effect.tryPromise / Effect.ensuring / Effect.acquireRelease, e.g. backlog/repository.ts:923-931 and dream/lock.ts:132-138. That works, but resource cancellation/cleanup discipline depends on each caller wrapping it correctly instead of the lock API making the safe pattern the default. This is not an immediate correctness break where callers already wrap carefully, but the review claim is accurate about the abstraction level.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Implemented Effect-native shared lock APIs in `extensions/tau/src/shared/lock.ts`: added `acquireSharedFileLockEffect`, `releaseSharedFileLockEffect`, and `acquireSharedFileLockScoped` (acquireRelease-based scoped lease). Refactored callers to consume the shared Effect API instead of hand-rolled wrappers: `extensions/tau/src/backlog/repository.ts` now uses `acquireSharedFileLockScoped` in `withWriteLock`, and `extensions/tau/src/dream/lock.ts` now uses `acquireSharedFileLockScoped`/`acquireSharedFileLockEffect`/`releaseSharedFileLockEffect` directly. Added regression tests in `extensions/tau/test/shared-lock-effect.test.ts` covering scoped release and non-scoped acquire/release helpers.
